name: Tactile Sensation Process
description: Tactile Sensation Process in Animals. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A40[Mechanical Stimulus] --> B40[Touch Receptor]
    B40 --> C40[Pressure Detection]
    B40 --> D40[Vibration Detection]
    B40 --> E40[Temperature Detection]
    C40 --> F40[Signal Generation]
    D40 --> F40
    E40 --> F40
    F40 --> G40[Sensory Neuron]
    G40 --> H40[Spinal Cord]
    H40 --> I40[Brain Processing]
    
    style A40 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B40 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C40 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style D40 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E40 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style F40 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style G40 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style H40 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style I40 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Tactile Sensation Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: The cellular and molecular basis of mechanosensation in mammals
The Neural Bases of Tactile Form and Texture Perception

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
